overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 5371 (day 28 partial @ prior run 04:18Z) -> 5580 (day 28 final, 24h later): +209 CVEs as NVD backfilled late Apr-28 publications. Extrapolation moved 5755 -> 5979 (+3.9%). Day-over-day rate normalizes April pace at ~199/day. Week-17 (Apr 20-26) closed final at 1469 (unchanged). Week-18 (Apr 27-May 03) partial through Apr-29 04:00Z: 428 entries. Counter-argument: extrapolation will continue to evolve as NVD backfills Apr-29/30 over the next week. Even at 5979 April lands meaningfully below March (6304) and within the Q1 churn band (4808-6304). No positive evidence of a Glasswing-driven volume shock; conversely, NVD publication latency 4-8 weeks means Mythos disclosures from April may not appear until mid-May at earliest.

kev_additions.reconciliation_note: CISA pushed catalog 2026.04.28 with 2 new entries (vs prior catalog 2026.04.24). Total entries 1583 -> 1585. April month-total 28 -> 30. Most recent dateAdded is 2026-04-28.
notable_entries: 2 new KEV entries today (2026-04-28): CVE-2024-1708 ConnectWise ScreenConnect (HTTP-adjacent path traversal — NP+DI candidate, not on watch list; flagged for analyst review).
CVE-2026-32202 Windows Shell (CWE-693 protection mechanism failure; auth bypass via input but desktop/local, not NP).

Watch list: 5 of 14 entries already in KEV (CVE-2026-39987 Marimo, -32201 SharePoint, -34197 ActiveMQ, -34621 Adobe Acrobat, -33825 Defender BlueHammer); 9 still 'watching' (Thymeleaf x2, wolfSSL x2, n8n, Cisco ISE x4, Cisco Webex). No watch list promotions this run.

claude_credited_known: CVE-2026-6746 / 6757 / 6758 - Firefox 150 (MFSA 2026-30, officially credited to Claude)

candidates_observed_today.note: Heap buffer overflow disclosed by calif.io with Claude as a tooling credit, NOT an Anthropic Mythos Preview attribution. Same pattern as CVE-2026-34197 (ActiveMQ Claude-assisted). Flagged for analyst review — does this category belong in claude_credited_cves or in a separate 'researcher-assisted' bucket? Currently NOT added.

glasswing_cves.vulncheck_baseline_unchanged: VulnCheck Garrity blog: 75 CVE records reference Anthropic since February (35 are Anthropic-tooling CVEs, 40 attributed to Anthropic researchers / affiliates). 28 of 40 are Firefox, 9 wolfSSL, 1 each F5 NGINX Plus / FreeBSD / OpenSSL. Counts unchanged today.
glasswing_cves.note: No new Anthropic-attributed Mythos Preview CVEs surfaced in last 24h. News cycle dominated by recap coverage. calif.io published a Claude-assisted nginx DAV bug (CVE-2026-27654) — flagged but not auto-added (researcher-assisted, not Mythos attribution). Public summary report from Anthropic still expected ~July 2026.

ms_patch_tuesday.source: Tenable blog, April 2026 Patch Tuesday roundup. BleepingComputer counterpoint: 167 flaws (different counting boundary). Continue to use 163 for consistency with prior values.
ms_patch_tuesday.note: No change since prior run - April Patch Tuesday was 2026-04-08, no new release until 2026-05-12. Note: Microsoft confirmed active exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 today, which lifts the count of actively-exploited April Patch Tuesday CVEs from 1 (SharePoint -32201) to 2.

rhel8_errata.source: stack.watch/product/redhat/enterprise-linux/ fetched 2026-04-29: 2026 YTD 115 vulnerabilities, average score 6.0. Q1 = 98, April delta = 17. RHEL aggregate counts unchanged from prior run.
rhel8_errata.note: April pacing remains below the 23-25/month Q1 average; final 2 days of April produced no new RHEL advisories.

github_poc.notes: PoC exploits circulating in researcher communities per CSO Online / The Hacker Wire 2026-04-17; not aggregated in public indexes yet
Same Thymeleaf cluster as CVE-2026-40477 — PoCs circulating
No dedicated PoC published to major indexes; researchers likely holding to allow patching window
Ni8mare: Cyera Research published technical writeup with attack chain; Orca Security, Picus, Upwind all published explainers. Working attack chain in the wild.

Sysdig Threat Research observed ITW exploitation within 9h 41m of disclosure WITHOUT public exploit code. Technical writeups (Resecurity, Security Affairs, Endor Labs, CSA Lab Space) contain sufficient detail for ad-hoc PoC reconstruction. Weaponized classification based on active ITW, not a published PoC.

google_news_hits.note: Spring security team disclosure; not AI-attributed but classic NP+DI candidates (SQL injection, JSONPath/SpEL injection in HTTP-facing component)

vendor_advisory_flags.notes: Insecure deserialization (CWE-502 — DI), HIGH severity, internal NVIDIA disclosure, no third-party credit. Pattern matches probable participant self-scan; NVIDIA is a Glasswing participant. Flagged for analyst review.
Companion CVE, also deserialization, CVSS 7.8. Same pattern. Flagged for analyst review.
Auth bypass via input (CWE-693), but desktop/Windows-Shell-local, not HTTP-adjacent. Does NOT meet 'HTTP-adjacent' criterion. Not flagged as participant self-scan.

np_di_candidates.rationale: Newly added to KEV 2026-04-28. CWE-22 path traversal in HTTP management console = textbook NP+DI. Note: 2024 CVE being added to KEV in 2026 indicates renewed exploitation campaign or rolling backlog reconciliation. The original SlashAndGrab (CVE-2024-1709/1708) cluster from Feb-2024 was Black Basta / Bl00dy ransomware — likely a retroactive KEV catalog cleanup rather than fresh exploitation. Either way it qualifies as NP+DI.

HIGH-severity, disclosed 2026-04-27. Vector store query injection via FilterExpressionConverter — keys and values not properly escaped. Spring AI runs as part of HTTP-facing Spring Boot apps; this is NP (Spring trust boundary) + DI (injection of crafted filter expressions). Fix: 1.0.6 / 1.1.5.
HIGH SQL injection in CosmosDBVectorStore.doDelete() via crafted document IDs. Disclosed 2026-04-27. NP+DI clean match. Fix: 1.0.6 / 1.1.5.

np_di_candidates.cwe: CWE-122 (heap buffer overflow), root cause CWE-191 (integer underflow)
np_di_candidates.rationale: Heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_dav_copy_move_handler via underflow in ngx_http_map_uri_to_path when Destination header shorter than location prefix. NP (HTTP server). Not classical DI but does involve untrusted-input-driven memory corruption. Disclosed by calif.io with Claude assistance (researcher-assisted, NOT Mythos Preview attribution).

Insecure deserialization (CWE-502) in NVIDIA BioNeMo. Internally-disclosed (no third-party credit) and NVIDIA is a Glasswing participant — fits probable participant self-scan profile. NP+DI by widened definition (deserialization is classical DI).

osv_scan.note: 0 fresh C/H advisories in last 72h across 50 high-priority NP packages (Maven/npm/PyPI/Go/RubyGems/crates.io). Most recent advisories on probed packages are 1-2 weeks old (urllib3 2026-03-13, etc.). No new library NP+DI candidates from OSV today. Spring AI Vector Store CVEs (2026-04-27 disclosure) didn't appear in OSV results because spring-ai is not on the canonical NP package list — added to np_di_candidates manually via news monitoring.

errors.detail: scripts/build_seven_year_npdi.py and scripts/build_cve_reference.py both hardcode CACHE=/sessions/bold-nice-euler/mnt/vulnerability\ analysis/cached-data, which is not mounted in the refresh agent's session. PermissionError on osv_cwe_results.json / spring_periodicity_data.json. This is identical to the path issue noted in CLAUDE.md ('iCloud Working Folder ...') and the prior 2026-04-28 refresh apparently committed past it. Data changes in this run do NOT touch seven-year-npdi-events.json or cve-reference.json; failure is unrelated to refresh deliverables. Recommend: parameterize the cache path or add a graceful no-op when cache is unavailable.
